description: In secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs line 422, put() does: let secret_bytes = value.as_bytes().to_vec(); This creates a plain Vec<u8> (not Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>) that holds the secret value before it is moved into the spawn_blocking closure. The bytes are zeroized inside the closure after create_object, but the initial heap allocation is not wrapped in Zeroizing. If the process is killed or panics after to_vec() but before the in-closure zeroization, the plain copy may linger in heap memory. Fix: change to let secret_bytes = Zeroizing::new(value.as_bytes().to_vec()); so the copy is zeroized regardless of how the closure exits.
title: pkcs11 put() copies secret bytes into plain Vec before spawn_blocking
